description: Regression floor for LongMemEval-S (18-item balanced pilot). Updates require an explicit `baseline-update:` commit-message tag; see docs/agent-memory/regression-protocol.md.
established_by: Pilot 8 — V3 prompt + multi-channel + synthesis + _abs-aware judge + anti-abstention prompt + numeric intent boost + temporal intent boost
notes: Update gate: nightly diff fails when any metric drops > tolerance below floor.

synth_abstain_rate is DIAGNOSTIC, not gating — a decrease is neutral-to-good (fewer wrong-abstains). Kept in metrics for visibility but the current check_baseline.rs treats every drop > tolerance as a fail, which is a false positive on this field. TODO(AM-1.8.b): teach check_baseline about direction-per-metric.

Per-category metrics are informational, not gating — single-session-assistant is a known structural 0/3 (extraction gaps). Even TwoPassExtractor in pilot 9 could not lift it — the synthesizer refuses to commit to assistant-stated facts.

The 0.089 gap from synth_judge=0.611 to the Phase 2 target of 0.70 is now believed to be an ARCHITECTURAL ceiling for this stack (Haiku extractor + BM25/vector recall + Haiku synthesis). Pilots 9/10/11 measured three orthogonal levers (TwoPassExtractor, v2 synth prompt, cross-encoder reranker) and none crossed the gap; the reranker (pilot 11) regressed to 0.389. See ROADMAP_AGENT_MEMORY.md "Pilot 8-11 results" for the full analysis.
